title: StorageBackend Phase 2: migrate L3 core read/write behind the seam (piggyback-only, do not schedule standalone)
current_state: agent/storage.py (T-165) defines the StorageBackend seam (SQLiteStorageBackend + InMemoryStorageBackend). Per its own docstring the migration is deliberately multi-phase: Phase 1 (DONE) - the T-186/T-205 conversation methods use the backend; Phase 2 (NOT done) - L3 core read/write methods in tools/tools_memory.py migrate one by one; Phase 3 (NOT done) - Supabase transport extracted behind a SupabaseBackend. This is intentional debt, filed so it is tracked instead of forgotten.
target_state: L3 core read/write paths go through the StorageBackend seam, executed opportunistically: whenever a ticket already touches an L3 method in tools/tools_memory.py, that method migrates in the same change. No standalone migration sprint - zero-behavior-change refactors of a working 2643-line module are churn. Phase 3 stays unscheduled until a second backend is actually needed.
migration_plan: 1. Standing rule, not a sprint: when any ticket touches an L3 read/write method in tools/tools_memory.py, move that method onto the backend seam in the same diff.
2. Each migrated method keeps its existing tests green and gains nothing else - no signature changes, no new parameters.
3. Track progress in this ticket's notes (methods done / remaining) as piggybacks accumulate.
4. Close when the L3 core set is through the seam; if a year passes with no ticket touching L3, close as wont-do with that finding - the seam earning no traffic is itself an answer.
5. python scripts/verify.py PASS on every piggyback; memory roundtrip contract tests stay green.
risk_notes: The trap here is scheduling this as dedicated work - a big-bang migration of a correct, tested module is negative-value churn (audit verdict: complexity is load-bearing). Piggyback or close, nothing in between. L3 is on the recurring write/read-divergence bug path: every migrated method needs its roundtrip test run, not just verify's syntax pass.
